definition: Countries promise to help each other.
example: Like best friends agreeing to always support each other.

definition: A written agreement between countries.
example: Like signing a rule sheet with your classmates.

definition: Punishments countries give another country without fighting.
example: Like being banned from playing a game because you broke the rules.

definition: How a country decides to act with other countries.
example: Like choosing how friendly you want to be with kids from another school.

definition: When a country chooses not to take sides in a conflict.
example: Like staying out of a fight between two friends.

definition: Two countries working together.
example: Like two classmates doing a project together.

definition: Many countries working together.
example: Like a group project with the whole class.

definition: Helping countries stop fighting and keep peace.
example: Like a teacher stopping students from arguing.

definition: A country’s office in another country.
example: Like having a small home for your team inside another school.

definition: A person who represents their country abroad.
example: Like a class representative speaking for the class.

definition: Countries becoming more connected.
example: Like students from different schools playing games together online.

definition: Influence through culture, ideas, or friendship.
example: Like making friends because you’re kind and fun.

definition: Influence using military or money pressure.
example: Like forcing someone to follow rules because you’re stronger.

definition: Talking to solve problems between countries.
example: Like two kids discussing how to share toys.

definition: Solving disagreements peacefully.
example: Like finding a fair solution when friends argue.

definition: Countries in the same area helping each other.
example: Like neighbors helping each other in a neighborhood.

definition: Helping another country with money or supplies.
example: Like giving food to a friend who forgot their lunch.

definition: Countries buying and selling goods.
example: Like swapping snacks with classmates.

definition: Countries working closely for long-term goals.
example: Like best friends planning many projects together.

definition: Countries creating rules for the world.
example: Like school rules made for all students.
